message:
workflows/runner: migrate the remaining inline workflow spawn to task::spawn_with_cx

One call site in `WorkflowRunner` was still using
`runtime_compat::task::spawn(async move { runner.run_workflow(...).await })`
and therefore not propagating a child Cx into the spawned workflow.
With `runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx` now available on both the
tokio and asupersync sides (previous commit), this last holdout can be
converted.

Change:

* crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs
  - Before spawning the workflow body, obtain a request-level Cx via
    `crate::cx::for_request()` and call
    `runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx(&request_cx, move |_child_cx| async move { ... })`.
    The closure binding is named `_child_cx` because the workflow
    body threads cx down its own dedicated channels; the point of
    this migration is not to start consuming it here, but to stop
    creating an orphaned spawn that sits outside the cancellation
    chain.

No new tests — coverage for the spawn_with_cx API was added in the
previous commit (`task_spawn_with_cx_receives_explicit_context`).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
